Candidates arriving more than half an hour after the examination has started shall not be allowed to participate, or may be admitted only at the discretion of the Chief Invigilator. [Source: Examination Regulations, Student General Conduct and Discipline]

assignment_and_coursework_rules.continuous_assessment_policies: Unapproved course registration will not be reckoned with for purposes of continuous assessment. [Source: Payment of Levies and Course Registration Guideline]
assignment_and_coursework_rules.coursework_requirements: Students are required to complete all laboratory practicals, assignments, projects, and other coursework as specified in individual course outlines. Unapproved course registration will not be reckoned with for coursework purposes. [INFERRED (coursework requirements component) — Common Nigerian Federal University Practice; registration component from Payment of Levies and Course Registration Guideline (2025/2026)]
assignment_and_coursework_rules.ca_exam_weighting: Continuous Assessment accounts for 30% and the final examination accounts for 70% of the total course grade. [INFERRED — Most common CA/exam weighting at UI consistent with NUC minimum guidelines; individual faculties may vary]

examination_rules.exam_eligibility: Unapproved course registration will not be reckoned with for examination purposes. Students must display their University Identity cards during each examination. [Sources: Payment of Levies and Course Registration Guideline; Examination Regulations, Student General Conduct and Discipline]
examination_rules.exam_hall_rules: Candidates may not leave the examination hall during the first and last half hour of the examination. Temporary leave is only allowed with permission and if accompanied by an invigilator. Lecture notes, textbooks, bags, and mobile telephones must not be brought into the examination hall. The use of scrap paper is not permitted; all rough work must be done in the answer booklet and crossed neatly through. Answer scripts or sheets, whether used or unused, should not be taken out of the examination hall. [Source: Examination Regulations, Student General Conduct and Discipline]
examination_rules.entering_or_leaving_exam_hall: Candidates must arrive 30 minutes before the start time. No candidate may leave during the first or last half hour. No candidate may enter more than 30 minutes after commencement, or may be admitted only at the Chief Invigilator's discretion. [Source: Examination Regulations, Student General Conduct and Discipline]
examination_rules.special_exam_conditions: Students who miss examinations due to documented illness must present a medical certificate from the University Health Service to the Faculty Examination Officer within 48 hours of the missed examination. A deferred or supplementary examination may be granted at the discretion of the appropriate university authority. [INFERRED — Common Nigerian Federal University Practice; consistent with standard UI Health Service and examination procedures]

academic_integrity.cheating_rules: While the examination is in progress, communication between candidates is strictly forbidden. Any candidate found giving or receiving irregular assistance may be required to withdraw from the examination. [Source: Examination Regulations, Student General Conduct and Discipline]
academic_integrity.plagiarism_rules: Plagiarism in any form, including copying from published sources, internet resources, or peers' work without proper attribution, is an academic offense at the University of Ibadan. Students found guilty of plagiarism in assignments, projects, dissertations, or theses may face penalties consistent with the examination misconduct framework, including suspension or expulsion. [INFERRED — Common Nigerian Federal University Academic Integrity Policy; consistent with UI's academic standards]
academic_integrity.exam_malpractice_policies: Examination misconduct or malpractice can lead to a student being expelled from the University. Investigations are processed formally through the Examinations Officer and the Faculty Student Disciplinary Committee. [Source: Examination Regulations, Student General Conduct and Discipline]
academic_integrity.penalties: Sanctions include Fines (Masters or Mistresses of Halls may impose fines of ₦1,000 or more), Disciplinary Probation, Suspension or Rustication, Expulsion, Loss of Privilege, and Reimbursement. [Source: Examination Regulations, Student General Conduct and Discipline]

discipline_system.warning_system: A written warning or letter of caution is issued for minor infractions or first-time offenses. Cumulative warnings may escalate to probation or rustication. The Central Student Disciplinary Committee (CSDC), chaired by the Vice-Chancellor, handles escalated cases of indiscipline. [INFERRED (warning issuance and escalation mechanism) — Common Nigerian Federal University Disciplinary Practice; CSDC reference from Examination Regulations, Student General Conduct and Discipline]
discipline_system.expulsion: Expulsion for examination misconduct or malpractice.
discipline_system.other_penalties: Fines (₦1,000 or more imposed by Masters or Mistresses of Halls), Loss of Privilege, Reimbursement.

student_conduct_rules.dress_code: Students should be in academic robes whilst meeting formally with the Vice-Chancellor. General campus dress expectations require decent and modest attire at all times; indecent or offensive dressing is not acceptable. [OFFICIAL (academic robes requirement): Examination Regulations, Student General Conduct and Discipline; general campus dress standard: INFERRED — Common Nigerian Federal University Campus Conduct Standard]
student_conduct_rules.electronics_policy: Mobile telephones must not be brought into the examination hall. [Source: Examination Regulations, Student General Conduct and Discipline]
student_conduct_rules.classroom_behaviour: Students must maintain orderly conduct during lectures. Mobile phones must be switched off or silenced during academic activities. Disruption of lectures or any academic activity in any form is prohibited and subject to disciplinary action. [INFERRED — Common Nigerian Federal University Classroom Conduct Standard]
student_conduct_rules.campus_behaviour: The Vice-Chancellor's Lodge is out of bounds to all students. Demonstrations and protests must be peaceful and orderly; demonstrating students must notify the authorities in writing at least 48 hours in advance about the nature, place, and time of the demonstration, ensuring no services are disrupted and no violence occurs. Locking, barricading, or vandalizing entry gates, streets, or offices is heavily sanctioned. [Source: Examination Regulations, Student General Conduct and Discipline]

student_welfare_rules.hostel_rules: Students must occupy only their officially allocated hostel room or bed space. Squatting and illegal swapping or sale of bed spaces are strictly prohibited. Hostel quiet hours must be observed. [INFERRED — Common Nigerian Federal University Hostel Regulations; consistent with standard UI Hall regulations]
student_welfare_rules.visitor_rules: Visitors are restricted to common areas within hostel premises. Cross-gender visits to hostel rooms are prohibited. Visitors must sign in at the porter's lodge and must leave the hostel before the designated curfew time (as determined by individual halls, typically 10:00 pm or 11:00 pm). [INFERRED — Common UI Hall Regulation Practice]
student_welfare_rules.cooking_policies: Cooking in hostel rooms is strictly prohibited. Students are directed to use university cafeterias or designated kitchen facilities. [INFERRED — Common Nigerian Federal University Hostel Policy; standard at UI]
student_welfare_rules.campus_safety: The University operates a Security Service that patrols campus 24 hours a day. Students are advised to report criminal activities or security concerns to the University Security Office or relevant university authority. [INFERRED — Common Nigerian Federal University Security Practice; consistent with known UI security arrangements]
student_welfare_rules.other_welfare_rules: The University Health Service provides medical care to students. Counseling services are also available through the Student Counseling Centre. Students are covered under health insurance arrangements accessible through the university's health services. [INFERRED — Common University of Ibadan student welfare services]

universities.unique_or_notable_rules: Course registration requires successful payment of all mandatory fees and levies, including the bundle fee or school fee, technology levy, utility levy, and where applicable, health or laboratory professional levies, before a student may proceed to course registration. [Source: Payment of Levies and Course Registration Guideline] The Central Student Disciplinary Committee (CSDC) is chaired by the Vice-Chancellor and handles individual cases of indiscipline. [Source: Examination Regulations, Student General Conduct and Discipline] Demonstrations and protests must be notified to authorities at least 48 hours in advance in writing. [Source: Examination Regulations, Student General Conduct and Discipline]

academic_structure.grade_letters_and_points: A = 5 (70–100%), B = 4 (60–69%), C = 3 (50–59%), D = 2 (45–49%), F = 0 (0–39%). Note: The pass grade of E (40–44%) is abolished in line with the NUC benchmark effective 2013/2014; the new minimum pass mark is 45% (D grade).
academic_structure.degree_classification: First Class Honours (4.50–5.00), Second Class Honours Upper (3.50–4.49), Second Class Honours Lower (2.40–3.49), Third Class Honours (1.50–2.39). Pass degrees (1.00–1.49) have been abolished. Professional programs (Medicine, Pharmacy) have a pass mark of 50%, except Dispensing and Forensic Pharmacy which is 60%.
academic_structure.credit_unit_requirements: 4-year programs: 120 units minimum; 5-year programs: 150 units minimum; 6-year programs: 180 units minimum. Sessional load: minimum 15 units, maximum 24 units per semester.

progression_and_academic_standing.academic_probation_rules: Any student who fails to attain a CGPA of 1.00 at the end of the session shall be required to repeat the year of study (probation). No student is allowed more than two periods of probation in their academic career.
progression_and_academic_standing.withdrawal_or_dismissal_conditions: If at the end of the probationary period the student still fails to attain a CGPA of 1.00, they shall be required to withdraw from the program. A student who has been on probation twice and still cannot meet the requirement must withdraw.
progression_and_academic_standing.carryover_or_failed_course_policies: A student must register for a failed course at the appropriate semester before registering courses for the current year of study. Final year students with a CGPA of 1.0 and above may sit for failed courses up to a maximum of 14 credit units per semester as external candidates without registering formally as regular students.
progression_and_academic_standing.course_repeat_rules: Failed courses must be repeated at the appropriate semester. Final year students meeting the CGPA threshold may sit as external candidates for up to 14 credit units.
progression_and_academic_standing.maximum_program_duration: An extension of not more than 50% of the normal period is allowed (e.g., a maximum of 6 years for a 4-year program).

attendance_policy.attendance_requirements: Attendance in all classes is required.
attendance_policy.minimum_attendance_percentage: 75% class attendance in a course is required to qualify to take examinations in that course.
attendance_policy.penalties_for_absence: Students who have not reached 75% class attendance in a course shall normally not qualify to take examinations in that course.

lateness_policy.lateness_to_lectures: Students arriving more than 15 minutes after the scheduled lecture start time may be marked absent for that session. [INFERRED — Common Nigerian Federal University Practice; consistent with general UNN academic culture]
lateness_policy.lateness_to_examinations: No candidate shall normally be allowed to leave the examination hall within the first 30 minutes, or to enter the examination hall after 30 minutes from commencement.

assignment_and_coursework_rules.continuous_assessment_policies: A minimum of two continuous assessment tests are administered per course per semester, consistent with NUC guidelines. Continuous assessment results are used to compute final grades where examinations are missed for valid reasons. [INFERRED (minimum two CA tests) — NUC Minimum Academic Standards; CA-for-grade-computation component from Undergraduate Academic Regulations (2013/2014)]
assignment_and_coursework_rules.coursework_requirements: Students are required to complete all assigned laboratory practicals, field trips, projects, and other coursework as specified in course outlines. Non-completion of required coursework may affect continuous assessment scores and examination eligibility. [INFERRED — Common Nigerian Federal University Coursework Requirement]
assignment_and_coursework_rules.ca_exam_weighting: Continuous assessment receives 30% weight; the semester examination receives 70% weight.

examination_rules.exam_eligibility: Students must have met the 75% attendance requirement. Students must sign their signature against their name and registration number in the attendance register during examinations.
examination_rules.exam_hall_rules: Students must sign the attendance register. No unauthorized materials are permitted.
examination_rules.entering_or_leaving_exam_hall: No candidate may leave within the first 30 minutes or enter after 30 minutes from commencement.
examination_rules.special_exam_conditions: Any student prevented from sitting an end-of-semester examination due to ill-health must submit a medical certificate from the Director of the University Medical Centre within 48 hours. If missed without valid reason, the continuous assessment record shall be used to return an appropriate grade. Final year students with a CGPA of 1.0 and above may sit for failed courses (up to 14 credit units) as external candidates.

academic_integrity.cheating_rules: Category D (Cancellation of paper): Copying or giraffing, exposing script, refusing to submit script, unruly behavior.
academic_integrity.plagiarism_rules: Plagiarism and academic fraud in projects, theses, assignments, and coursework are prohibited at UNN. Such offenses are treated as academic dishonesty and may attract penalties consistent with the examination malpractice categorization system, ranging from course failure and suspension to expulsion depending on the severity. [INFERRED — Common Nigerian Federal University Academic Integrity Framework; consistent with UNN's broader examination malpractice policy]
academic_integrity.exam_malpractice_policies: Category A (Expulsion): Impersonation, exam leakage, smuggling scripts, physical assault, bringing weapons or guns into the hall. Category B (Suspension 1 Session + Rustication from hostel): Procuring fake medical certificates, lobbying for grades, bringing unauthorized materials into the hall. Category C (Step down 1 semester + Rustication from hostel): Mutilation of registration forms, forcing entry while unregistered. Category D (Cancellation of paper): Copying or giraffing, exposing script, refusing to submit script, unruly behavior.
academic_integrity.penalties: Penalties range from cancellation of paper, 1 semester suspension, 1 session suspension, and rustication from hostel, to expulsion. Cases are investigated by an examination malpractice committee.

discipline_system.warning_system: A written warning or letter of caution is issued for minor infractions or first-time offenses. More serious offenses are escalated to the Examination Malpractice Committee or the appropriate disciplinary body. [INFERRED — Common Nigerian Federal University Disciplinary Practice; consistent with UNN's tiered malpractice response system documented in the Undergraduate Academic Regulations]
discipline_system.probation: Academic probation (repeat year) for CGPA below 1.00.
discipline_system.suspension: 1 semester suspension or 1 session suspension depending on the category of offense.
discipline_system.expulsion: Expulsion for Category A offenses (impersonation, exam leakage, smuggling scripts, physical assault, weapons in hall).
discipline_system.other_penalties: Cancellation of paper (Category D); rustication from hostel (Categories B and C); step down one semester (Category C).

student_conduct_rules.dress_code: Students are expected to dress decently and modestly on campus at all times. Indecent dressing, including skimpy, tattered, transparent, or revealing clothing, is prohibited. [INFERRED — Common UNN and Nigerian Federal University Dress Code Policy]
student_conduct_rules.electronics_policy: Mobile phones and unauthorized electronic devices are not permitted in the examination hall. Possession of such devices during examinations constitutes a Category B malpractice offense and attracts suspension for one session plus rustication from the hostel. [INFERRED — Consistent with UNN's documented Category B malpractice rules and common Nigerian federal university electronics policy]
student_conduct_rules.classroom_behaviour: Students must maintain orderly conduct during lectures. Mobile phones must be switched off or silenced. Disruptive behavior, noise-making, and non-academic activities during class are prohibited. [INFERRED — Common Nigerian Federal University Classroom Conduct Standard]
student_conduct_rules.campus_behaviour: Cultism and secret society activities are strictly banned at UNN and attract severe disciplinary action including expulsion. Unauthorized demonstrations, violence, vandalism, and campus disorder are prohibited. Students are expected to resolve disputes through official university channels. [INFERRED — Consistent with UNN's publicly known stance on cultism and campus peace, and common federal university policy]

student_welfare_rules.hostel_rules: Students must occupy only their officially allocated hostel room or bed space. Squatting and illegal swapping or sale of bed spaces are strictly prohibited. Hostel quiet hours must be observed. [INFERRED — Common Nigerian Federal University Hostel Regulations; consistent with general UNN hostel management practice]
student_welfare_rules.visitor_rules: Visitors are restricted to common areas within hostel premises. Cross-gender visits to hostel rooms are prohibited. Visitors must leave the hostel before the designated curfew time (typically 10:00 pm). [INFERRED — Common Nigerian Federal University Visitor Policy]
student_welfare_rules.cooking_policies: Cooking in hostel rooms is strictly prohibited. Students are directed to use designated kitchens or university cafeterias. [INFERRED — Common Nigerian Federal University Hostel Policy; standard practice at UNN]
student_welfare_rules.campus_safety: UNN operates a University Security Service that patrols campus 24 hours a day. Students are advised to report suspicious activities or security threats to university authorities rather than directly to external law enforcement. [INFERRED — Common Nigerian Federal University Security Practice]
student_welfare_rules.other_welfare_rules: UNN provides a University Medical Centre for student healthcare, a Student Counseling Centre, and sports facilities. Students are encouraged to utilize the Medical Centre for all health-related matters. The Handouts Policy also serves as a student welfare measure: no lecturer shall force or penalize any student for failing to purchase handouts or textbooks. [INFERRED (health center, counseling, sports) — Common UNN and Nigerian Federal University Welfare Services; Handouts Policy from Undergraduate Academic Regulations (2013/2014)]

universities.unique_or_notable_rules: Handouts Policy: The university administration discourages the production and sale of handouts. No lecturer shall force or penalize any student for failing to purchase handouts or textbooks. Aegrotat Degree: An unclassified degree awarded on compassionate grounds to a final year student unable to complete examinations due to permanent disability, provided their prior academic work was of passing standard. Second Bachelor's Degree: A former graduate of the University may pursue a second bachelor's degree with a minimum attendance period of one session.
